editor_prompt_text:
You are the Whetstone editor.
Apply or decline feedback according to the spec authority model.

Return only a single JSON object matching editor_summary.json.
Do not return markdown, explanation, fenced code, an editor object, or a decisions array.
The top-level object MUST set round_number to 1.
The draft_before_hash MUST be 3cdba751fe524aceeb4d9a8a4fea0b0470b1dc033846b7f1f61e1014f4bcf6c5.
The draft_after_hash MUST be 3cdba751fe524aceeb4d9a8a4fea0b0470b1dc033846b7f1f61e1014f4bcf6c5.
This live round is capture-only; do not assume the repository draft is mutated by this prompt.
In capture-only mode, feedback may be accepted in substance while its issue remains unresolved because no draft text changed.
The top-level object MUST include exactly these fields:
- round_number
- draft_before_hash
- draft_after_hash
- accepted_feedback_ids
- modified_feedback_ids
- declined_feedback
- created_conflict_ids
- resolved_issue_ids
- unresolved_issue_ids

Use accepted_feedback_ids for feedback accepted as written.
Use modified_feedback_ids for feedback accepted in substance but implemented differently.
Use declined_feedback only for feedback declined under the allowed decline taxonomy.
Use resolved_issue_ids only when the current draft_after resolves the issue.
Use unresolved_issue_ids for issues still unresolved in the current draft_after.
If there are no entries for an array field, return an empty array for that field.

Reviewer feedback JSON:
{
  "draft_hash": "3cdba751fe524aceeb4d9a8a4fea0b0470b1dc033846b7f1f61e1014f4bcf6c5",
  "feedback": [
    {
      "affected_sections": [
        "tiny-spec"
      ],
      "authority_impact": null,
      "baseline_severity": "major",
      "claim": "The Tiny Spec is too incomplete to support convergence checking; it contains only a single hashing statement and omits required normative details (scope/purpose, definitions, inputs/outputs, required behavior, and error/edge-case handling).",
      "determinism_impact": "minor",
      "evidence": "Draft contains only: \"# Tiny Spec\" and \"## Hashing\" with the single rule \"The system hashes the draft.\"",
      "feedback_id": "fb_0001",
      "in_scope": true,
      "invariant_violated": null,
      "issue_fingerprint": "1111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111",
      "issue_id": "iss_0f1e2d3c4b5a6978",
      "issue_type": "spec_completeness",
      "normalized_severity": "major",
      "oscillation_key": {
        "concern_type": "completeness_gap",
        "direction": "add",
        "fingerprint": "ac217fbe586f6f3d3d9ff16fbe63b0811750fa4589376b3f7e4f66e30d5d0d04",
        "opposition_key": "f55bca3dc034b56f54fae8d235004069552ccd554328ff5e6de4db31a243a005",
        "scope": "structural",
        "section_id": "tiny-spec"
      },
      "recommended_change": "Add normative sections that define: (1) what the \"system\" is and when hashing occurs, (2) what a \"draft\" is (exact bytes/encoding), (3) required behavior and acceptance criteria, and (4) error handling and edge cases. Ensure each requirement is testable and unambiguous.",
      "rubric_impact": null,
      "severity_rationale": "With only one vague sentence, independent implementations cannot converge on intended behavior, and reviewers cannot evaluate completeness or correctness."
    },
    {
      "affected_sections": [
        "tiny-spec-hashing"
      ],
      "authority_impact": null,
      "baseline_severity": "major",
      "claim": "The hashing requirement is non-deterministic/under-specified because it does not define the hash algorithm, canonicalization, encoding, or output format.",
      "determinism_impact": "major",
      "evidence": "The only hashing rule is: \"The system hashes the draft.\"",
      "feedback_id": "fb_0002",
      "in_scope": true,
      "invariant_violated": null,
      "issue_fingerprint": "2222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222222",
      "issue_id": "iss_89abcdef01234567",
      "issue_type": "hashing_determinism",
      "normalized_severity": "major",
      "oscillation_key": {
        "concern_type": "determinism_violation",
        "direction": "constrain",
        "fingerprint": "61a642dce7e2e2955183a56fe36591ad1d97e959c7cd26d5847808d09a6582c9",
        "opposition_key": "52b1db7e9fc50cf62ef5bab85fbf19b30edf67c6e2531fa237e35f454435a26e",
        "scope": "local",
        "section_id": "tiny-spec-hashing"
      },
      "recommended_change": "Constrain the hashing spec by defining: the exact algorithm (e.g., SHA-256), the exact input byte sequence (including normalization/canonicalization rules and text encoding), and the output representation (hex/base64, lowercase/uppercase). State that hashing is deterministic over identical canonical inputs.",
      "rubric_impact": null,
      "severity_rationale": "Different implementations will choose different algorithms/encodings/canonicalizations, producing different hashes for the same draft, preventing convergence."
    }
  ],
  "profile": "convergence_strict_check",
  "reviewer": {
    "model": "gpt-5.2",
    "name": "Whetstone Reviewer",
    "version": "1.0"
  },
  "round_number": 1
}

Draft:
# Tiny Spec

## Hashing

The system hashes the draft.
prompt_text:
You are the Whetstone reviewer.
Review profile: convergence_strict_check
Phase: phase_2

Return only a single JSON object. Do not return markdown, explanation, or fenced code.
The top-level object MUST match reviewer_feedback.json and include:
- round_number: 1
- profile: convergence_strict_check
- reviewer: object with concrete name, version, and model strings
- draft_hash: 3cdba751fe524aceeb4d9a8a4fea0b0470b1dc033846b7f1f61e1014f4bcf6c5
- feedback: array of feedback items, or [] if no issues are found

Each feedback item MUST represent exactly one issue and include all required schema fields.
The reviewer MUST author issue_id and issue_fingerprint as stable identifiers for the item.
issue_id MUST match iss_[16 lowercase hex characters], for example iss_0123456789abcdef.
issue_fingerprint MUST be exactly 64 lowercase hex characters.
draft_hash MUST exactly equal the draft_hash value supplied above.
Severity fields MUST use only blocker, major, minor, nit, or null. Do not use high, medium, low, none, n/a, or similar aliases.
Each feedback item MUST include exactly these base fields:
- feedback_id
- issue_id
- issue_fingerprint
- issue_type
- affected_sections
- baseline_severity
- authority_impact
- determinism_impact
- rubric_impact
- normalized_severity
- invariant_violated
- claim
- evidence
- recommended_change
- in_scope
- severity_rationale

Phase 2 reviewer prompt requirements:
- Phase 2 oscillation classification is mandatory for every feedback item.
- The full canonical classification table appears below.
- The reviewer MUST choose from the listed concern_type, direction, and scope enums.
- The reviewer MUST NOT invent categories or leave oscillation_key fields blank.
- section_id MUST be chosen from the canonical section IDs listed below.
- The reviewer MUST NOT author fingerprint or opposition_key fields.
- Use modify only when no more specific direction applies.

Every feedback item MUST include oscillation_key with section_id, concern_type, direction, and scope only.
Whetstone computes fingerprint and opposition_key after validating the section_id.
Choose the nearest enum value.

Canonical section IDs:
- tiny-spec
- tiny-spec-hashing

Concern types:
- clarity_gap: section is ambiguous, undefined, or unclear
- completeness_gap: section is missing required content, rule, definition, or edge case
- consistency_violation: section contradicts another section or prior decision
- determinism_violation: section permits non-deterministic behavior
- authority_violation: section violates a defined role, boundary, or authority rule
- scope_violation: section addresses something out of scope or omits something in scope
- redundancy: section duplicates content elsewhere
- precision_gap: section is correct but under-specified

Directions:
- add: add missing content or behavior
- remove: remove content or behavior
- modify: change content when no more specific direction applies
- clarify: clarify ambiguous content without materially changing scope or strictness
- constrain: make behavior stricter, narrower, or more bounded
- relax: make behavior looser, broader, or less restrictive

Scope values:
- local: specific rule, sentence, definition, field, or invariant
- structural: section organization, responsibility boundary, lifecycle shape, or cross-section architecture

Symmetric opposition pairs:
- add/remove: add <-> remove
- constrain <-> relax

Examples:
- {"section_id":"oscillation-detection-full-definition","concern_type":"determinism_violation","direction":"constrain","scope":"local"}
- {"section_id":"artifact-schemas-minimum-required-fields","concern_type":"completeness_gap","direction":"add","scope":"local"}

Rubric:
(no rubric provided)

Draft:
# Tiny Spec

## Hashing

The system hashes the draft.
